blurb: Already, Paul insists, your life has been lifted and hidden with Christ in God. The rest of today—its errands, its worries—cannot add or subtract from that concealment; it is finished treasure kept in an unbreakable vault. So set your mind there first, before the noise of duty rises, and let every earthly task become a quiet echo of the place where you already belong.

verses.text: If ye then be risen with Christ, seek those things which are above, where Christ sitteth on the right hand of God.
Set your affection on things above, not on things on the earth.
For ye are dead, and your life is hid with Christ in God.
When Christ, who is our life, shall appear, then shall ye also appear with him in glory.
